title: Frankfort-on-the-Main
content:
В В В В пїЅSankt Martin war ein frommer Mann
В В В В Trank gerne Cerevisiam,
В В В В Und hatt er kein Pecuniam
В В В В So liess er seinen Tunicam.пїЅ

В В В В (Comment by Herr Schwackenhammer.)

В В В В Vonce oopon a dimes in Frankfort der Herr Breitemann exsberiencet an interfal pedween de periot ven he hat gespent de last remiddance he hat become from home, und de arrifal of de succedin wechsel, or bill of exghangeпїЅund, in blain derms, was hard up. Derefore he vent to dat goot relation who may pe foundt at den or fifdeen per cent all de worlt ofer,пїЅпїЅmine Onkel,пїЅпїЅund poot his tress-goat oop de shpout for den florins. No sooner vas dis done, dan dere coomed an infitation from de English laity in whom he vas so moosh mit lofe in betaken, to geh mit her to a ball-barty. Awful bad vas he veel, und sot apout tree hours mitout sayin nodings, und denn wafin his hand, boorst out mit de vollowin version of dat peaudiful lied by Wilhelm Caspary:пїЅ

В В В В пїЅMein Frack ist im Pfand-haus.пїЅ

В В В В Mine tress-goat is shpouted, mine tress-goat aint hier,
В В В В Vhile you in your ball-ropes go splurgin, mein tear!
В В В В To barties mit you IпїЅm infitet you know,
В В В В Boot my pest coat ish shpoutedпїЅmine poots are no go.
В В В В To hell mit mine OnkelпїЅdat rasgally knafe!
В В В В Dis pledgin und pawnin has mate me his slafe!
В В В В Ven I dink of his sign-bost, den dree dimes I bawl,
В В В В Vhile mine plack pants hang lonely und dark on de wall.

В В В В Goot night to dee fine lofeпїЅso lofely und rich,
В В В В Mein tress-goat ish shpoutedпїЅgon-fount efery stitch!
В В В В I dinks dat olt Satan troo all mine affairs,
В В В В Lofe, business, und fun, has peen sewin his tares.
В В В В My tress-goat ish shpoutedпїЅmine tress-goat aint here,
В В В В While you in your glorie go shinin, mein tear,
В В В В Und de luck of der teufel ish loose ofer all,
В В В В Vhile my black pants hang lonely und dark on de wall.

В В В В Dis four-goin song vas over-set by der Hans Breitmann from de German of Wilhelm Caspary, whose lyric vas a barody on a dranslation made indo Deutsch by Freiligrath from anoder boem py Sir Waldherr Scott, vitch Sir Waldherr vas kit de idпїЅe of from an oldt Scottish ballad vitch pegin mit de vortsпїЅ

В В В В пїЅMy hearts in de Hielands, mein hearts ish nae hier,
В В В В Mein hearts in de Hielands, in wilden revier;
В В В В It hoonts for de shtag, und id hunts for de reh,
В В В В Mein hearts ist im Hochland wo immer ich geh.пїЅ

В В В В Dis is de original Scotch, as goot as I can mineself rememper it. Ven I vas dell der Herr Karl Blind pout dis intercommixture of perplexified dransitions from Scotch to English, and dence into German, and dereafter into a barody, vitch vas be done ofer again indo Herr BreitmannпїЅs own slanguage, he sait it vas a RattenkпїЅnigпїЅa phrase too familiar to mine readers to require any wider complication.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Charles G. Leland represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Charles G. Leland's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
